description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline that uses the python:3 Docker image and defines two stages, test and deploy, with a global before_script running `pip install --editable .`. In the test stage add a job named test that runs only on tags, executes `pip install -U setuptools wheel`, `python setup.py bdist_wheel`, `pip install dist/*.whl`, `mv dist/*.whl ./`, and `gitlab-release --link-artifact --description "https://pypi.python.org/pypi/gitlab-release" ./*.whl`, then publishes the generated .whl file as an artifact. Also in the test stage add a manual job called gitlab-release-test with environment gitlab-release-test that runs `git config --global user.email "ci@gitlab.com"`, `git config --global user.name "Gitlab Release"`, `pip install requests setuptools_scm`, `gitlab-release --version`, clones the repository using the $gitlab_release_test_token, runs `bash ./create_update.sh`, captures the next version from version.txt, and calls `python gitlab_release.py` twice with appropriate server, project_id, release_tag, and token arguments. In the deploy stage add a job named deploy that runs only on tags and performs `pip install -U twine setuptools wheel`, `python setup.py bdist_wheel`, and `twine upload -u $PYPI_USER -p $PYPI_PASS dist/*` to publish the wheel to PyPI. Ensure the test job’s artifact path is set to `./*.whl`.
